text:
too many clothes
sometimes she had more clothes than she needed. Now she didn’t have quite enough. She had kept them in buckets, sometimes, in her house - returning to and from stores not even bothering to look at what she’d bought. She knew for a fact, now that she had at this moment a full laundry basket in the trunk of her car full of all sorts of patterns textures and tones making a swirl of color that would never be seen by anyone as she could not - never would  drop it off at the vintage store that chose clothing - that would go through it, untangle the mess - drop each time individually into a receptacle behind the desk, perhaps scoff at the mismatched assortment,

No, she had not enough clothes now - perhaps an extra set of pants might be of use to her - she could wear them as it had started to rain but what if the boy came out of his house just when she went home to change - there she could be - reaching into her trunk for an extra pair of pants - just so she could be warm - selfishly and meanwhile he’d choose that second to stop playing his guitar probably. He played only sad ballads the kind she couldn’t think to like, though recently, maybe, what with the sad weather and everything - so much more alive to her now that she was part of it - could practical be said to be raining down from the sky herself and if the rain water happened upon a couple of extra drops that glanced now down her cheeks - who would be the wider - not even the rain would know the reason why there was suddenly more of it, though such a minuscule amount that it shouldn’t care about the extra.

Almost noontime or so she would imagine had she been able to see the sky clearly beyond the clouds for she had come to know the time of day from the sun - a sun dial - finally but there was something so desperate about this extra knowledge - the way it had come to her - not from knowing it in a book - the accumulation of a series of rather desperate actions.

She passed by the window - that tiny chink of light that she could see through - she had to estimate that he was still sleeping - and here when it was almost noon. She was almost ashamed of his behavior for a moment a sound like a shrill in her head passed through her - the yell if she were in the house (as she had been that one day. Invited in - a guest - a visitor - offered water even - allowed to sit on the couch - how had she not taken the moment to examine every inch of the couch that would be useful now

It was starting to get squelchy in her shoes - when she walked she was accompanied by an extra noise - an odd sound like air being sucked out of the sky - a little minute vacuum “squelch squelch squelch” she wondered was she now an electric thing - an appliance of sorts - like a refrigerator that hummed so you knew if it was still plugin.
It was awfully tiring walking around this alley - pretending to be on some important mission or another - she sat down. One of the neighbors had unloaded a large pile of leaves - perhaps they had been

collecting patterns Collecting patterns → the pattern collector “oh - that’s an example of something I was thinking about → some people collect more patterns than others → you start collecting patterns when there is an unanswered question in your brain
When you encounter a question that you don’t know the answer to out in the world, this sets in motion a condition called “priming” - which is a bit like a mechanism that gets activated if and only if - an example/evidence to answer the question gets encountered in the world. For example: Am I feeling sad today? (event: had a sad thought, had a happy thought) @@PB@@ Another reason to start collecting patterns is if something with something in common happens twice (something out of the ordinary)
For example: guy threw bones at me on the pch, guy was flicking people off on the pch

@@PB@@ a new game that I just made up (and I think it will make into the “notebook”) The goal of this game is to assign yourself an exercise that is just out of reach. The procedure of this game is not to try to get it “right” or “conquer the problem of the game” but instead to let yourself drown a little under it (This I liken to that experience i had with the slipped knot (it slipped off my finger) @@PB@@

Rather than recognizing that it’s not going to work, and changing course, reconsidering your approach, instead, walk straight into failure until what you’re building or painting becomes a bloody mess. As though you are a computer, and you are simply carrying out the commands of a program that is using you to run.

Of course, at some point you may become so entangled in the mess you have made for yourself, that you will need to make some decisions to get out of the debacle as there will be nowhere to go. Or you have maybe hit a well. This is where it gets interesting (at least in my opinion). Keep going. find a way, or find places where you can continue your aforementioned program. If you are sewing edges together, and the edges do not meet at the places you expected them to - just keep sewing. Find a different edge to sew onto - sometimes that makes some amount of sense with the original assignment if you are pairing a puzzle and suddenly you realize, just keep adding details to the part that already exists. Let the part you need to add warp to fit the space that still exists, even painting in between other parts - finding white space even if it no longer is continuing to look more and more like the photograph you were documenting.

As you do this, your mind will decide that you cannot quite keep doing this, at least not infinitely. And (hopefully) your subconscious mind will make all the reasons for you - will find a way to make sense of things - not to fix it mind you. But only to make sense of it. (diagram - garnet, painting etc. idk) Anyway - when you sew it together it’s not gonna work :( ( 🙂)...because the facets, well it’s sort of an indirect translation - like - you can’t translate stone to fabric without accounting for the different material…

oh and the other one is Normally, painting a painting is not like a puzzle…but this time it is. Find a photograph that has so many complicated details (in the background)...don’t even worry about the foreground maybe - just find a picture where there’s a bunch of distractions in the background. And then kind of get really into these distractions. Oh - this is another assignment. I think it there is no white space (room for leniency)

that means, if you don’t connect everything right - because things are not the right proportion, or they are in slightly the wrong places (or both), than the painting won’t make sense, and you’ll reach a point where the original photograph is so far from what you are actually painting - for example - you will come upon someone’s leg, and see that in the photo, there should be a face, smiling, next to it. But you have made the leg so small that the face is about an inch away, when, according to the photograph, they should be touching. Now, you have a couple of options. How are you going to solve this problem?

“Fun w/ Rules and Restrictions”
Sometimes we create rules for ourselves based on games that we are not playing anymore. As a child, maybe it was important not to talk so that your family wouldn’t scream. Maybe you are no longer playing that game anymore. And now you sometimes find yourself being quiet in certain kinds of situations, and you don't now why.

“What’s in the magic chest” - “I’m going on a picnic” sort of like a spirograph. That makes designs based on what it is set to do, you could, without looking at the (spirograph), set it or let it go, and, based on what pattern it produced, guess or estimate, what was on the spirograph. In the same way, we can access the “periphery” of our mind/In the same way, we can find out what rules we are following unconsciously. observing the patterns of our behavior (and tracing it back to why we may be “choosing” these actions.

PART II I am unsure how common this experience is. At least for me, if I let my mind start thinking without telling it where to go, it will often, I can sometimes look at a material (think about a material or I get an idea for a sculpture. I start designing it in my head. And then - it does not feel like a process of creation or invention but instead, of trying to walk in a room when I’m blind - when I first wake up in the morning, and I do not want to open my eyes (to go to the bathroom) or close the blinds). Ok, I am sensing the room - trying to figure out what things are in it, based on whatever various senses I may still have - I can feel with my feet - that I am on the carpet by the bathroom. My hands - stretched out - tell me that the wall which leads to the second room in my house is starting. And in my mind, if I d not talk, just listen, I can feel something starting to develop - a picture of the sculpture that I want to make - in my mind.

There are certain decisions that need to be made. For example - if there is a string - where will it connect to. I have lots of little pieces of wood. I could nail them all together. But a thin piece of metal - to hold them together - now that doesn’t seem like it would do the trick. A thin little closure is not (?) (in line with) the feeling I am feeling. Instead I would like to use a swatch of fabric.

I could nail it on - no - that is too - rambunctious? - jarring? - rapid? But give itself will not feel like it will hold, even if it will. I settle on staples. they are thin enough, and I like how wide they are, that they encompass a greater selection of the side of the fabric, they go right up by the edge, so you know the edge is there - it emphasizes the edge. I could hem the edge - but no - I think I like it frayed - the hem says I tried really hard to hide what it really looks like. The fray says “I don’t really mind if you see what is here I have nothing to hide.”

And that is how I feel as a person - I have nothing to hide. Or at least I want to have nothing to hide :) But I do not want the frays to be “too big” - so as to call attention to themselves. That would say: “Look at me!” I care about what you think. And I want you to see me in a certain way.” So the frays will have to stay small, which means the piece will have to not be handled a lot - and that is fine by me. I can not handle the piece a lot - I don’t need to touch things, in fact, I don’t want to. I am scared a lot of the time - trying not to touch things anyway - so why not have something try on.

“Even in my mind, after I am done thinking about the piece, I will try not to touch the frays so they don’t get emboldened. It means that I cannot think about the piece too much, or when I do, I have to be in a “careful” mindset - I want to touch things with gloves, when I am not breathing accidentally.

As you can see, this is a process, primarily, of feeling and not thinking. Rather than guiding your mind, as you might do normally, or otherwise, let it be guided by whatever is going on in the background - let it “see” the rules that you cannot see yourself - on the periphery. And let yourself see them there - through the piece you made - what were you following/. And if possible, then when you are ready, you can think about “why” - you can look at the piece that you made (in your mind), and describe to yourself why you made all the choices you did.

Each “action” - each choice was a decision, and if you were not guiding you’re mind, you were instead letting it be guided by some other part - your mind, but not your conscious - you get an insight in to your subconscious - what did your mind pick for you - staples instead of a nail - that is because you did not want to be too rambunctious - too jarring - too violent. Oh - and also so that it would not just connect it in one place but would be a more seamless connection - right by the edge, so you could pretend the edge was not there if you wanted to..

“observational drawing as a system of movement”
PAIRINGS
“I thought
it was you
in the hall - no, only your
footsteps: blue and
powder blue
TRYING to see what your mind might be thinking underneath :) (!)
they go together like wine and cheese…together, you can imagine, some reason for why they could be together - what they have in common

And as for the first part of the exercise: the photos are about what is found in the background. Blow it up - emphasize the details - even if they are blurry - ambiguous. A reference to when we remember something, and - oh that small detail meant something I didn’t even think about till now (know I needed)...it’s blurry/fuzzy because some parts of our mind we cannot quite remember what happened, and yet - they are useful to think about anyway. Is the blurry/fuzzy part because we don’t know what happened, or because we don’t know what it meant. Primarily, these are from memories I had forgotten about. Is the blurry/fuzzy part because it’s not in focus or it’s too far in the background or because it’s in motion? Oh actually also from

“How much strength is this gonna take?” opening up a bag of avocados (there’s actually a picture anyway - this is about the sayings project - taking random thoughts that I have in my head about super mundane things, and elevating their importance into the realm of poetry so that they are very valuable. This is the same game as finding pictures that were randomly taken and zooming in on the background, and making it square and black and white so it gains importance - becomes “high art” - putting things on a pedestal (picture of a pedestal) (pic) - - not trash exactly, but random things - things you have throughout the day - these could be more important than you think. And they do make ? pictures… 🙂

Rules on the Periphery Moses got the 10 commandments because he couldn’t remember them when he wasn’t in the right mindset. We give ourselves rules when we know we won’t always be in this mental state - “don’t eat sugar, exercise everyday,” when we are focused on our long term goals vs. wallowing in sadness “oh yes - my sensible self said this” and I trust her, even though I do not understand her currently” We can set rules for others who are too young to understand - don’t pee in the pool - or don’t have the same morals

side note (?) -> we can refer back/get back to any mind set, by seeing one of the patterns we collected. pieces of evidence we picked up and trying to remember why it was important to us - drawer pull THEN: we can turn our pattern collecting habits into a rule and try to collect the patterns of a specific collector even if we don’t know why ex. things that made me smile. Things that are beautiful. Things that I like about myself (or: things that made me angry. things that i don’t like about other people - (these are in a third realm where we do not know if they’d be helpful/ they might be deceptively helpful

Anyway - the concept it son the periphery of our thoughts, not in the middle, so when we use SET theory - it accesses that part of our brain, and if I do so kind of like the decision pipeline, because,: only at certain junctures do we even consider the “rules”, and for this, we access the {patterns} that are on the periphery of our imagination.

Collecting Competing (/Contradictory) Patterns (diagram) ← people as paradoxes nasty things that auntie florence and shayna did “your outfit is too christian” - “wow, you look very jewish” (and I remember what I was wearing lol “Oh - you didn’t know about using the bracket as a way to…” (when correcting my essay) “Oh you never thought of using aesthetic packaging) (when explaining about how I used to just put stuff in an envelope. “Wow, you’re shirt is very, very political,” → this ended the pattern and now they’re not catty to me anymore - I just need to be catty back for some reason. …Anyway once you collect these competing patterns, it’s good cause a) it’s an example of paradoxes, and b) you can practice having a more nuanced version of people

META so basically, you want to: design a game to produce/induce manual labor - something that involves multiple steps, something that starts to feel like a ritual, with lots of rules, so you know what to do when you are stuck…

Systems of Movement CHANGE THE SCALE/PERSPECTIVE Sometimes, when I’m feeling weird about my life, I zoom in or out depending on what most effectively and beneficially changes my perspective. if you imagine something larger, you’ll get to see more details, and, if you imagine it smaller, it’s easier to see the bigger picture and take in all of what’s there. I sometimes like to imagine that humans are ants -w hen I look on the weather app, and see how specifically we’ve documented and predicted everything about our planet - I often think of ants, carefully recording the humps and divots of a giant crumb they’ve found. To them, that is home. And, were to simply pick it up, all the ants would have to shift. Then I imagine some giant human picking up our planet so all the people vacationing in Majorca suddenly see the sun going behind a cloud and they all take their towels in and cover up their bathing suits, and go home.

Rules on the Periphery Anyway, point being - when we’re not in the “right” mindset - something is off - we can’t think clearly, or we’re lying to ourselves on purpose, as part of a game, as part of instrumentalism - it’s important to have -> the rules we went in with, so we can get back to our original mindset if we need to…r
